steps.message: Block the source IP associated with the XXE attack in the WAF/firewall to prevent further external entity injection attempts, as per general web application security best practices for containing input-based attacks.

Isolate the server that processed the malicious XML to prevent further lateral movement or data exfiltration, given the XXE payload attempted to read a system file (`/etc/hostname`), which aligns with WAHH 8.7.1 indicating potential data disclosure.

Initiate a vulnerability scan across the application to identify other 'functionality-specific input vulnerabilities' and 'identify specific application functions where these defects are liable to arise', as advised in WAHH 8.5 and 8.7.1 regarding XXE.

Review and approve the proposed configuration change to disable DTD processing and external entity resolution in XML parsers to prevent future XXE attacks. This is a critical step in mitigating the 'external entity injection' vulnerability described in WAHH 8.7.1 and requires strategic authorization due to potential application impact.

Deploy the patch or configuration update to disable external entity processing (e.g., configuring XML parsers to disallow DTDs or external entity resolution) in the affected application, directly addressing the XXE vulnerability as per WAHH 8.7.1.

Update the incident management system with containment and remediation details, and notify relevant stakeholders in the #security-incidents channel about the successful handling of the XXE vulnerability as part of the closure process.

params.message: XXE incident PB-XXE-001 contained and remediated. Further details available in the incident ticket. Verification scan initiated.
